lines.line: # Introduction to the Oral History Project

> The speaker, Hannah Hobbs, introduces herself as an undergraduate student conducting an oral history interview with Mr. George Crocombe as part of a project in partnership with the Idaho State Historical Society. The purpose of the interview is to capture Mr. Crocombe's unique perspective on the Treasure Valley and its history in the seed industry, as well as the changes he has witnessed throughout his life.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Oral History Project
metadata.synopsis: The speaker, Hannah Hobbs, introduces herself as an undergraduate student conducting an oral history interview with Mr. George Crocombe as part of a project in partnership with the Idaho State Historical Society. The purpose of the interview is to capture Mr. Crocombe's unique perspective on the Treasure Valley and its history in the seed industry, as well as the changes he has witnessed throughout his life.

lines.line: # Childhood in Idaho

> The speaker reminisces about their childhood in Idaho, highlighting the abundance of open spaces and outdoor activities that were a significant part of their upbringing. They reflect on the lack of video games and television, which led to children creating their own entertainment, and they consider this a valuable experience that may be lacking for some children today.

metadata.title: Childhood in Idaho
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reminisces about their childhood in Idaho, highlighting the abundance of open spaces and outdoor activities that were a significant part of their upbringing. They reflect on the lack of video games and television, which led to children creating their own entertainment, and they consider this a valuable experience that may be lacking for some children today.

lines.line: # Family Activities and Community Involvement

> The speaker discusses their family's active lifestyle, including participation in various sports and outdoor activities. They also mention their family's involvement in the local Presbyterian Church and other community organizations, which were often related to the interests and activities of the family members.

metadata.title: Family Activities and Community Involvement
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses their family's active lifestyle, including participation in various sports and outdoor activities. They also mention their family's involvement in the local Presbyterian Church and other community organizations, which were often related to the interests and activities of the family members.

lines.line: # Meeting the Spouse and Family Life

> The speaker shares the story of how they met their wife at the University of Idaho and the serendipitous encounters that led to their relationship. After college, they spent time in Australia before getting married and starting a family, which the speaker describes as a beautiful journey.

metadata.title: Meeting the Spouse and Family Life
metadata.synopsis: The speaker shares the story of how they met their wife at the University of Idaho and the serendipitous encounters that led to their relationship. After college, they spent time in Australia before getting married and starting a family, which the speaker describes as a beautiful journey.

lines.line: # Early Involvement with Crookham Company

> The speaker describes their early involvement with the family business, Crookham Company, from playing in the seed factory as a child to working in various seasonal operations starting at the age of 13. They detail the progression of their responsibilities within the company and the influence of their family members who also worked there.

metadata.title: Early Involvement with Crookham Company
metadata.synopsis: The speaker describes their early involvement with the family business, Crookham Company, from playing in the seed factory as a child to working in various seasonal operations starting at the age of 13. They detail the progression of their responsibilities within the company and the influence of their family members who also worked there.

lines.line: # Joining the Family Business

> The speaker explains the decision to join the family business, acknowledging the expectations set by their family but also their own enjoyment of the work. They discuss the natural progression from working in various roles within the company to eventually becoming CEO, a position they have held for many years.

metadata.title: Joining the Family Business
metadata.synopsis: The speaker explains the decision to join the family business, acknowledging the expectations set by their family but also their own enjoyment of the work. They discuss the natural progression from working in various roles within the company to eventually becoming CEO, a position they have held for many years.

lines.line: # College Experience and Its Influence

> The speaker talks about the expectation of attending college within their family and the impact of their education at the University of Idaho on their personal and professional development. They emphasize the importance of higher education in providing the foundation to tackle challenges and figure things out in life.

metadata.title: College Experience and Its Influence
metadata.synopsis: The speaker talks about the expectation of attending college within their family and the impact of their education at the University of Idaho on their personal and professional development. They emphasize the importance of higher education in providing the foundation to tackle challenges and figure things out in life.

lines.line: # Experiences Abroad in Norway and Australia

> The speaker credits their parents for encouraging exposure to different cultures, which led to their experiences living abroad in Norway and Australia. They discuss the impact of these experiences on their ability to adapt and learn, as well as the influence of their mother's Norwegian heritage on their time spent in Norway.

metadata.title: Experiences Abroad in Norway and Australia
metadata.synopsis: The speaker credits their parents for encouraging exposure to different cultures, which led to their experiences living abroad in Norway and Australia. They discuss the impact of these experiences on their ability to adapt and learn, as well as the influence of their mother's Norwegian heritage on their time spent in Norway.

lines.line: # Becoming CEO of Crookham Company

> The speaker reflects on the unexpected transition to becoming CEO of Crookham Company after their father's retirement. They discuss the challenges and responsibilities that came with the role and the gradual process of taking over the company's leadership alongside their sister.

metadata.title: Becoming CEO of Crookham Company
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on the unexpected transition to becoming CEO of Crookham Company after their father's retirement. They discuss the challenges and responsibilities that came with the role and the gradual process of taking over the company's leadership alongside their sister.

lines.line: # Importance of Family Ownership

> The speaker emphasizes the importance of maintaining Crookham Company as a family-owned business, despite the challenges that come with balancing family dynamics and business decisions. They share their perspective on the complexities of running a family business and the efforts to keep family gatherings separate from business discussions.

metadata.title: Importance of Family Ownership
metadata.synopsis: The speaker emphasizes the importance of maintaining Crookham Company as a family-owned business, despite the challenges that come with balancing family dynamics and business decisions. They share their perspective on the complexities of running a family business and the efforts to keep family gatherings separate from business discussions.

lines.line: # Crookham Company's Global Footprint

> The speaker outlines Crookham Company's international presence, including ownership of facilities in the United States and Australia. They discuss the company's attempt to establish a direct presence in the Netherlands, the challenges faced, and the subsequent shift to a dealer-based model for European markets.

metadata.title: Crookham Company's Global Footprint
metadata.synopsis: The speaker outlines Crookham Company's international presence, including ownership of facilities in the United States and Australia. They discuss the company's attempt to establish a direct presence in the Netherlands, the challenges faced, and the subsequent shift to a dealer-based model for European markets.

lines.line: # Global Agricultural Production

> The speaker provides insights into the historical role of the Netherlands as a seed trading nation and the importance of the Treasure Valley as a significant vegetable seed production area. They compare agricultural practices in other countries, emphasizing the need for smart growth and preservation of farmland, drawing parallels with their own experiences in the Treasure Valley.

metadata.title: Global Agricultural Production
metadata.synopsis: The speaker provides insights into the historical role of the Netherlands as a seed trading nation and the importance of the Treasure Valley as a significant vegetable seed production area. They compare agricultural practices in other countries, emphasizing the need for smart growth and preservation of farmland, drawing parallels with their own experiences in the Treasure Valley.

lines.line: # Treasure Valley's Growth and Agriculture

> The speaker expresses concerns about the rapid growth in the Treasure Valley and its negative impact on agriculture. They advocate for smart growth that respects agricultural land and the needs of farmers, sharing personal experiences of conflict with new developments and the challenges of farming in a changing landscape.

metadata.title: Treasure Valley's Growth and Agriculture
metadata.synopsis: The speaker expresses concerns about the rapid growth in the Treasure Valley and its negative impact on agriculture. They advocate for smart growth that respects agricultural land and the needs of farmers, sharing personal experiences of conflict with new developments and the challenges of farming in a changing landscape.

lines.line: # Farm Labor Shortage in the Treasure Valley

> The speaker confirms the existence of a farm labor shortage in the Treasure Valley, which is a widespread issue affecting various aspects of agriculture. They note that this is not a unique problem to Idaho, as other countries are facing similar challenges in securing agricultural labor.

metadata.title: Farm Labor Shortage in the Treasure Valley
metadata.synopsis: The speaker confirms the existence of a farm labor shortage in the Treasure Valley, which is a widespread issue affecting various aspects of agriculture. They note that this is not a unique problem to Idaho, as other countries are facing similar challenges in securing agricultural labor.

lines.line: # Utilizing the H-2A Work Visa Program

> The speaker discusses the company's research into the H-2A work visa program for hiring foreign workers. They explain that the program is not cost-effective for their specific needs due to the short duration of their labor requirements. The speaker also clarifies that they do not own the land but work with local farmers for seed production.

metadata.title: Utilizing the H-2A Work Visa Program
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the company's research into the H-2A work visa program for hiring foreign workers. They explain that the program is not cost-effective for their specific needs due to the short duration of their labor requirements. The speaker also clarifies that they do not own the land but work with local farmers for seed production.

lines.line: # Exporting Goods Internationally

> The speaker explains the process of exporting goods internationally, which involves assessing the ability to ship seeds to a country, determining if the market can afford their products, and establishing trust with local partners. They describe the gradual development of international relationships and the importance of trust in business dealings.

metadata.title: Exporting Goods Internationally
metadata.synopsis: The speaker explains the process of exporting goods internationally, which involves assessing the ability to ship seeds to a country, determining if the market can afford their products, and establishing trust with local partners. They describe the gradual development of international relationships and the importance of trust in business dealings.

lines.line: # Domestic vs. International Sales

> The speaker reveals that a significant portion of their business is international, accounting for 40% of sales. They discuss the implications of this on the company's operations and how a shift to domestic-only sales would require downsizing and restructuring. The speaker also touches on the evolution of the company from a production-focused entity to one that is driven by its own genetics and breeding program.

metadata.title: Domestic vs. International Sales
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reveals that a significant portion of their business is international, accounting for 40% of sales. They discuss the implications of this on the company's operations and how a shift to domestic-only sales would require downsizing and restructuring. The speaker also touches on the evolution of the company from a production-focused entity to one that is driven by its own genetics and breeding program.

lines.line: # Conclusion of the Interview

> The speaker concludes the interview by expressing satisfaction with the conversation and the range of topics covered. They acknowledge that there may be additional thoughts that come to mind later, but they appreciate the opportunity to share their experiences and insights during the interview.

metadata.title: Conclusion of the Interview
metadata.synopsis: The speaker concludes the interview by expressing satisfaction with the conversation and the range of topics covered. They acknowledge that there may be additional thoughts that come to mind later, but they appreciate the opportunity to share their experiences and insights during the interview.

# Childhood in Idaho

> The speaker reminisces about their childhood in Idaho, highlighting the abundance of open spaces and outdoor activities that were a significant part of their upbringing. # Early Involvement with Crookham Company

> The speaker describes their early involvement with the family business, Crookham Company, from playing in the seed factory as a child to working in various seasonal operations starting at the age of 13. They detail the progression of their responsibilities within the company and the influence of their family members who also worked there.

They discuss the natural progression from working in various roles within the company to eventually becoming CEO, a position they have held for many years.
# College Experience and Its Influence

> The speaker talks about the expectation of attending college within their family and the impact of their education at the University of Idaho on their personal and professional development. They emphasize the importance of higher education in providing the foundation to tackle challenges and figure things out in life.
# Experiences Abroad in Norway and Australia

> The speaker credits their parents for encouraging exposure to different cultures, which led to their experiences living abroad in Norway and Australia. They discuss the impact of these experiences on their ability to adapt and learn, as well as the influence of their mother's Norwegian heritage on their time spent in Norway.
# Becoming CEO of Crookham Company

> The speaker reflects on the unexpected transition to becoming CEO of Crookham Company after their father's retirement. They discuss the challenges and responsibilities that came with the role and the gradual process of taking over the company's leadership alongside their sister.
# Importance of Family Ownership

> The speaker emphasizes the importance of maintaining Crookham Company as a family-owned business, despite the challenges that come with balancing family dynamics and business decisions. They share their perspective on the complexities of running a family business and the efforts to keep family gatherings separate from business discussions.
# Crookham Company's Global Footprint

> The speaker outlines Crookham Company's international presence, including ownership of facilities in the United States and Australia. They discuss the company's attempt to establish a direct presence in the Netherlands, the challenges faced, and the subsequent shift to a dealer-based model for European markets.
# Global Agricultural Production

> The speaker provides insights into the historical role of the Netherlands as a seed trading nation and the importance of the Treasure Valley as a significant vegetable seed production area. They compare agricultural practices in other countries, emphasizing the need for smart growth and preservation of farmland, drawing parallels with their own experiences in the Treasure Valley.
# Treasure Valley's Growth and Agriculture

> The speaker expresses concerns about the rapid growth in the Treasure Valley and its negative impact on agriculture. They advocate for smart growth that respects agricultural land and the needs of farmers, sharing personal experiences of conflict with new developments and the challenges of farming in a changing landscape.
# Farm Labor Shortage in the Treasure Valley

> The speaker confirms the existence of a farm labor shortage in the Treasure Valley, which is a widespread issue affecting various aspects of agriculture. They note that this is not a unique problem to Idaho, as other countries are facing similar challenges in securing agricultural labor.
# Utilizing the H-2A Work Visa Program

> The speaker discusses the company's research into the H-2A work visa program for hiring foreign workers. They explain that the program is not cost-effective for their specific needs due to the short duration of their labor requirements. The speaker also clarifies that they do not own the land but work with local farmers for seed production.
# Exporting Goods Internationally

> The speaker explains the process of exporting goods internationally, which involves assessing the ability to ship seeds to a country, determining if the market can afford their products, and establishing trust with local partners. They describe the gradual development of international relationships and the importance of trust in business dealings.

> The speaker reveals that a significant portion of their business is international, accounting for 40% of sales. They discuss the implications of this on the company's operations and how a shift to domestic-only sales would require downsizing and restructuring. The speaker also touches on the evolution of the company from a production-focused entity to one that is driven by its own genetics and breeding program.

> The speaker concludes the interview by expressing satisfaction with the conversation and the range of topics covered. They acknowledge that there may be additional thoughts that come to mind later, but they appreciate the opportunity to share their experiences and insights during the interview.

lines.line: # Introduction to the Oral History Project

> The speaker introduces themselves as Hannah Hobbs, an undergraduate student conducting an oral history interview in partnership with the Idaho State Historical Society. The interview is part of a project guided by Professor Rachel Miller and aims to capture the speaker's unique perception of the Treasure Valley and its history in the seed industry.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Oral History Project
metadata.synopsis: The speaker introduces themselves as Hannah Hobbs, an undergraduate student conducting an oral history interview in partnership with the Idaho State Historical Society. The interview is part of a project guided by Professor Rachel Miller and aims to capture the speaker's unique perception of the Treasure Valley and its history in the seed industry.

lines.line: # Childhood Memories and Family Background

> The speaker reminisces about their childhood in Idaho, highlighting the open spaces and outdoor activities that were a significant part of their upbringing. They describe their family as active and involved in various activities, including church and community clubs. The speaker also mentions their family's connection to the Steunenberg family, notable figures in Idaho's history.

lines.line: # Early Involvement with Crookham Company

> The speaker discusses their early involvement with Crookham Company, a family-owned business. They recall playing in the seed factory as a child and starting to work there at the age of 13. The speaker's sisters were also involved in the business, with one serving as CFO and the other in the marketing department.

They recall playing in the seed factory as a child and starting to work there at the age of 13. lines.line: # Decision to Join the Family Business

> The speaker reflects on their decision to join the family business, acknowledging an expectation to do so but also a personal choice based on enjoyment of the work. They discuss the transition to CEO after their father's retirement and the challenges of taking on new responsibilities within the company.

metadata.title: Decision to Join the Family Business
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on their decision to join the family business, acknowledging an expectation to do so but also a personal choice based on enjoyment of the work. They discuss the transition to CEO after their father's retirement and the challenges of taking on new responsibilities within the company.

lines.line: # Importance of Keeping the Business Family-Owned

> The speaker emphasizes the importance of keeping Crookham Company family-owned, despite the challenges that come with balancing family dynamics and business decisions. They share an anecdote about how family gatherings often turned into business discussions, highlighting the deep connection between family and the company.

metadata.title: Importance of Keeping the Business Family-Owned
metadata.synopsis: The speaker emphasizes the importance of keeping Crookham Company family-owned, despite the challenges that come with balancing family dynamics and business decisions. They share an anecdote about how family gatherings often turned into business discussions, highlighting the deep connection between family and the company.

lines.line: # International Business and the Netherlands Office

> The speaker clarifies that while Crookham Company owns several locations, including in Australia, they do not own property in the Netherlands. They discuss the challenges of managing a business remotely and the decision to shift their business model in the Netherlands to work through a local dealer.

metadata.title: International Business and the Netherlands Office
metadata.synopsis: The speaker clarifies that while Crookham Company owns several locations, including in Australia, they do not own property in the Netherlands. They discuss the challenges of managing a business remotely and the decision to shift their business model in the Netherlands to work through a local dealer.

lines.line: # Agricultural Production in the Netherlands and Worldwide Seed Market

> The speaker provides insight into the Netherlands' historical role as seed traders and the importance of the Treasure Valley as a significant vegetable seed production area. They discuss the global reach of their products and the challenges of navigating international markets, including the importance of understanding local agricultural practices.

metadata.title: Agricultural Production in the Netherlands and Worldwide Seed Market
metadata.synopsis: The speaker provides insight into the Netherlands' historical role as seed traders and the importance of the Treasure Valley as a significant vegetable seed production area. They discuss the global reach of their products and the challenges of navigating international markets, including the importance of understanding local agricultural practices.

lines.line: # Impact of Treasure Valley's Growth on Agriculture

> The speaker expresses concern about the rapid growth in the Treasure Valley and its negative impact on agriculture. They advocate for smart growth that respects agricultural land and practices, drawing comparisons to how other countries prioritize farmland over development.

metadata.title: Impact of Treasure Valley's Growth on Agriculture
metadata.synopsis: The speaker expresses concern about the rapid growth in the Treasure Valley and its negative impact on agriculture. They advocate for smart growth that respects agricultural land and practices, drawing comparisons to how other countries prioritize farmland over development.

lines.line: # Farm Labor Shortage

> The speaker confirms a shortage of farm labor in the Treasure Valley, a challenge that is not unique to Idaho but is experienced in various agricultural regions around the world. They discuss the difficulties in finding reliable labor and the impact this has on agricultural operations.

metadata.title: Farm Labor Shortage
metadata.synopsis: The speaker confirms a shortage of farm labor in the Treasure Valley, a challenge that is not unique to Idaho but is experienced in various agricultural regions around the world. They discuss the difficulties in finding reliable labor and the impact this has on agricultural operations.

lines.line: # Use of H-2A Work Visa Program

> The speaker discusses the company's research into the H-2A work visa program for hiring foreign workers. They explain that the program does not align with their seasonal labor needs due to the minimum employment duration required, and therefore, they do not utilize H-2A workers.

metadata.title: Use of H-2A Work Visa Program
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the company's research into the H-2A work visa program for hiring foreign workers. They explain that the program does not align with their seasonal labor needs due to the minimum employment duration required, and therefore, they do not utilize H-2A workers.

lines.line: # Exporting Goods Internationally

> The speaker outlines the process of exporting goods internationally, which includes assessing the ability to ship seeds to a country, determining if the market can afford their products, and establishing trust with local partners. They describe the gradual development of international business relationships and the importance of mutual trust.

metadata.title: Exporting Goods Internationally
metadata.synopsis: The speaker outlines the process of exporting goods internationally, which includes assessing the ability to ship seeds to a country, determining if the market can afford their products, and establishing trust with local partners. They describe the gradual development of international business relationships and the importance of mutual trust.

lines.line: # Hypothetical Scenario of Domestic-Only Sales

> The speaker considers the hypothetical scenario of Crookham Company only selling within the United States. They explain that such a change would significantly downsize the company, reducing the need for extensive facilities and personnel. The speaker also touches on the evolution of the company's business model from producing for others to focusing on their own genetics and products.

lines.line: # Closing Remarks

> The speaker concludes the interview by expressing satisfaction with the way the conversation unfolded. They acknowledge that while they may think of additional points to share later, they appreciate the opportunity to participate in the oral history project.

> The speaker reminisces about their childhood in Idaho, highlighting the open spaces and outdoor activities that were a significant part of their upbringing. story.description: The interview with George Crookham focuses on his personal and professional experiences in the Treasure Valley, Idaho, including his work with Crookham Company, a family-owned seed business established in 1911. Crookham reflects on his upbringing, family dynamics, and education, and shares his journey from college at the University of Idaho to traveling and living abroad in Norway and Australia. He discusses his rise to CEO and describes challenges in maintaining a family-owned business amidst company growth, international expansion, and local demographic changes. The interview also touches on relationships in international trade, the importance of agriculture in the region, and the impact of urban development on farming.
story.title: Crookham_George_OH2809 Hist305
